input.description: Two vehicles collided at a four-way intersection; front-end damage to the insured's sedan, airbags deployed.
Insured's parked car was stolen overnight from the driveway; police report filed the same morning.
A rock kicked up on the highway cracked the windshield; no other damage to the vehicle.
Grease fire in the kitchen caused smoke and burn damage to cabinets, countertops, and the ceiling.
A pipe burst under the kitchen sink and flooded the basement, soaking flooring and drywall.
Burglars forced the back door and stole a television, laptops, and jewelry from the home.
A delivery visitor slipped on the insured's icy front walkway and fractured a wrist.
Policyholder was hospitalized for three days after a severe allergic reaction; itemized medical bills submitted.
A hailstorm dented the roof, damaged gutters, and broke two skylights.
Insured was rear-ended at a stoplight; bumper and trunk crumpled, no injuries reported.
Catalytic converter and wheels stripped from the insured's truck in a parking garage overnight.
Side window of the car shattered by a flying object; glass-only claim, no break-in.
An electrical fault in the garage sparked a fire that spread to the attic and damaged the roof structure.
Water heater failed and leaked for hours, ruining the laundry room floor and adjacent carpet.
Home broken into through a window while the family was away; cash and electronics taken.
A contractor working on the roof fell from the insured's scaffolding and was injured on the property.
Insured received emergency surgery after a fall at home; submitting hospital and physician invoices.
High winds from a storm tore shingles off the roof and toppled a fence.
Multi-car pileup on the freeway; insured's vehicle sustained rear and side impact damage.
Vehicle reported stolen from a shopping mall lot; keys were not left in the car.
Windshield chip expanded into a long crack; replacement requested, no collision involved.
Candle left burning ignited curtains and damaged the living room wall and furniture.
Dishwasher supply line ruptured, flooding the kitchen and seeping into the subfloor.
Garage broken into overnight; power tools and two bicycles stolen.
A guest's child was bitten by the insured's dog during a backyard gathering, requiring stitches.
Policyholder underwent an MRI and physical therapy after a slip; submitting medical expense receipts.
Lightning strike during a thunderstorm damaged the roof and fried home electronics.
Insured sideswiped a guardrail avoiding debris; driver-side panels scraped and dented.
Locked car stolen from outside the insured's apartment; recovered later stripped of parts.
Frozen pipe in an exterior wall split and leaked behind the drywall, causing water staining.
Fender-bender repair on a sedan.
input.facts: Body shop quote $3,200. Policy deductible $500. Fully covered, no betterment.

input.description: Roof hail damage.
input.facts: Contractor estimate $12,000 replacement cost. Recoverable depreciation $2,000 is held back until repairs complete. Deductible $1,000. Initial payment only.

input.description: Total-loss sedan.
input.facts: Actual cash value $11,800. Deductible $1,000. Insured elects to keep the salvage, valued at $1,500, which is deducted.

input.description: Basement water loss.
input.facts: Mitigation $2,400 plus repairs $5,600 (total $8,000). Of that, $1,800 is mold remediation, but the policy mold sublimit caps mold at $1,000. Deductible $1,000.

input.description: Stolen contents.
input.facts: Scheduled electronics and jewelry claimed at $7,500 replacement. Non-scheduled items depreciated 20% from $7,500. Deductible $500.

input.description: Windshield replacement.
input.facts: OEM glass and labor $1,150. Glass deductible waived under the policy.

input.description: Kitchen fire.
input.facts: Repair estimate $18,400. Deductible $2,500. Additional living expenses of $1,600 covered separately and included in this payout.

input.description: Vehicle total loss with prior damage.
input.facts: ACV $16,000 but adjuster applies a $1,200 prior-damage deduction. Deductible $1,000. Sales tax of 6% on the adjusted ACV is added back to the payment.

input.description: Wind-damaged fence and shed.
input.facts: Fence $3,000 and shed $4,000, both on a replacement-cost basis. Combined depreciation 15%. Deductible $1,000.

input.description: Rear bumper repair.
input.facts: Estimate $1,900. Deductible $500.

input.description: Burst pipe, multiple rooms.
input.facts: Flooring $6,200, drywall $3,300, cabinetry $4,500. The cabinetry has 25% depreciation applied; the rest is replacement cost. Deductible $1,000.

input.description: Hail-dented vehicle, repairable.
input.facts: Paintless dent repair $4,800. Deductible $500. A 10% betterment is deducted for new trim.

input.description: Smoke damage, contents and structure.
input.facts: Structure cleaning $9,000. Contents $6,000 at replacement cost less 30% depreciation. Deductible $1,500.

input.description: Glass and side mirror.
input.facts: Door glass $420 and mirror $280. Deductible $100.

input.description: Roof replacement after storm.
input.facts: Replacement cost $20,000. Roof is 12 years old; policy pays actual cash value with 40% depreciation. Deductible $2,000.

input.description: Liability settlement for a slip-and-fall.
input.facts: Medical specials $8,200, lost wages $3,500, general damages estimated at 1.5x specials. The policy has a $25,000 per-occurrence limit which is not exceeded.

input.description: Theft of a scheduled watch.
input.facts: Scheduled item valued at $9,000 with an agreed-value endorsement (no deductible, no depreciation).

input.description: Multi-coverage auto loss.
input.facts: Collision repair $7,400 (deductible $1,000) plus a rental reimbursement of $40/day for 12 days. Both paid together.

input.description: Frozen-pipe water damage.
input.facts: Repairs $5,000. Deductible $1,000. Insurer applies a 10% non-maintenance reduction to the post-deductible amount.

input.description: Minor collision.
input.facts: Estimate $2,600. Deductible $1,000.

input.description: Total loss with loan payoff.
input.facts: ACV $13,500. Deductible $500. The insured has gap coverage; the loan balance is $15,000, so gap pays the $2,000 shortfall in addition to the ACV settlement.

input.description: Wind and water combined.
input.facts: Wind portion $4,000 (deductible $1,000) and water portion $3,000 (separate $500 deductible). Both covered.

input.description: Contents with category sublimits.
input.facts: Claimed: $4,000 jewelry (sublimit $2,500), $2,000 electronics, $1,500 clothing. Deductible $500.

input.description: Door ding and panel.
input.facts: Estimate $1,250. Deductible $250.

input.description: Hotel and meals after a covered loss.
input.facts: Additional living expense: 14 nights at $180/night plus $700 in meals. No deductible on ALE.

input.description: Partial roof with overhead and profit.
input.facts: Base repair $10,000, plus 10% overhead and 10% profit applied sequentially. Deductible $1,000.

input.description: Stolen vehicle recovered, damaged.
input.facts: Repairs after recovery $3,800. Personal items in the car $600 (contents). Deductible $500 applies once to the auto portion only.

input.description: Cracked bumper cover.
input.facts: Estimate $900. Deductible $250.

input.description: Fire with code-upgrade coverage.
input.facts: Direct fire damage $30,000. Building code upgrades required $5,000, covered up to a 10%-of-loss ordinance sublimit. Deductible $2,500.

input.description: Water loss with depreciation holdback.
input.facts: Repairs $8,000 replacement cost; depreciation holdback 20% recoverable on completion. Deductible $1,000. Initial actual-cash-value payment only.
